desc: Bergen is Norway's rain-washed fjord gateway, framed by seven mountains and a historic harbor city center. It combines UNESCO heritage, strong seafood culture, and easy access to dramatic coastal landscapes, with highest visitor pressure in long-day summer months and calmer shoulder-season value in spring/autumn.
hls: Major departure point for fjord cruises
Compact center with walkable harbor core

todo.name: Wander Bryggen wooden wharf
todo.desc: Explore narrow passageways, artisan shops, and Hanseatic-era structures that define Bergen’s maritime identity.

todo.name: Ride the Fløibanen to Mount Fløyen
todo.desc: Take the funicular for city-and-fjord panoramas, then continue on forest trails if weather allows.

todo.name: Bergen Fish Market lunch
todo.desc: Sample local seafood such as fish soup, shrimp, and salmon near the harborfront stalls.

todo.name: Troldhaugen (Grieg Museum)
todo.desc: Visit composer Edvard Grieg’s home and concert hall for a quieter cultural stop outside the core center.

todo.name: Day cruise to Mostraumen or Hardanger
todo.desc: Use Bergen as a launch point for narrow fjords, waterfalls, and steep mountain walls.

todo.name: Ulriken cable car and ridge walk
todo.desc: Go up Mount Ulriken for higher alpine-style views and, in good weather, traverse back toward Fløyen.

wifi.note: Excellent across city hotels and cafés, with reliable remote-work performance in central districts.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick-Bergen nonstop service is often seasonal; many itineraries connect via Oslo, Amsterdam, or Copenhagen. Return fares are often ~£80-£170 off-peak, ~£170-£290 in shoulder demand, and ~£290+ in peak fjord season.
prac.visa: Norway is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Frequent rain and wind can affect fjord cruise schedules and mountain cable-car operations.
Summer accommodation near Bryggen and the harbor can book out early during cruise-heavy periods.
Weather can change quickly in a single day — bring waterproof layers even in peak season.

sweet: May-August is usually best for long-day hiking and fjord access, while September can offer better value with still-good daylight.
